blockingIssues.description: src/app/MockAppStateProvider.tsx lockProfile and clearCredentials paths do not clear runtimeEventLog, so stale event-log entries can bleed across lock/clear transitions in demo/mock mode.

src/screens/DashboardScreen/panels/EventLogPanel.tsx keeps clearedBaselineSeq until entry.seq exceeds pre-clear max, but clearRuntimeEventLog resets seq to 0; post-clear events are hidden until seq surpasses the old maximum.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a specific worker checklist item for AppState/MockAppStateProvider parity: when introducing a new AppState slice, update both lockProfile and clearCredentials reset paths in MockAppStateProvider.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m4-event-log-buffer added runtimeEventLog state but omitted reset logic in MockAppStateProvider lock/clear mutators, causing state bleed in demo mode.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen ui-screen-worker scrutiny expectations to require explicit agent-browser evidence for user-visible changes, or require a clearly justified exception path in handoff validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two m4-eventlog reviews observed UI features validated only with tsc/vitest despite AGENTS.md and ui-screen-worker guidance requiring agent-browser for UI validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: Agent-browser requirement mismatch was reported by both m4-event-log-panel and m4-event-log-filter-and-scroll reviews.
